scope: src/agents_sync/{daemon,archive,portable_archive,filesystem_lock,filesystem_windows_retry,rules_io,slash_command_io}.py + tests/test_daemon_signals.py + tests/test_portable_archive.py + tests/test_filesystem_retry_helper.py + tests/test_rules_io.py + tests/test_rules_sync.py + tests/test_slash_command_*.py
verdict_rationale: The single CRITICAL finding (CQ-01 silent-swallow + blocking-sleep daemon) and both MAJOR findings (CQ-03 daemon-loop coverage gap, CQ-04 non-transactional import) are closed with substantive remediation; residuals are bounded WARNING/INFO items that do not threaten the data-preservation invariant.
remediation_diff_summary: Phases 1.1–4 rewrote daemon.py around a threading.Event-driven cancellable sleep and a real consecutive-failure budget; added four daemon loop tests; restructured import_from_zip into explicit Phase A (stage to .import_pending_<uuid>/) → Phase B (os.replace promotion) → Phase C (projection + state.json), and strengthened the AC-10 test to assert no live canonicals and no pending leftovers. CQ-07 unlink and CQ-08 mkdir are now wrapped in retry_fs. Shotgun duplication of the per-tool partition (CQ-11) is extracted into canonical.apply_per_tool_partition() and consumed in all three sites.
detailed_analysis: daemon.py grew from 37 to 108 lines. The bare `except: continue` with time.sleep ignoring stop has been replaced by a typed three-outcome dispatch: clean / per-pair-failed / whole-poll exception, each advancing or resetting a consecutive_failures counter, with explicit max-failures exit code 1. Cancellable sleep is now stop.wait(interval_seconds); shutdown latency is bounded by ~0 instead of the prior 30s. test_daemon_signals.py grew from one helper assertion to four behavioural tests using real threading.Event and a scripted FakeSyncer. import_from_zip is now organised into three labelled phases: Phase A saves every accepted canonical into state_dir/.import_pending_<uuid>/ via save_canonical_to; if any save raises, pending dir is shutil.rmtreed and live tree is never touched. Phase B promotes pending files into canonical/ via os.replace inside a try/finally. Phase C runs projection and only then save_state. AC-10 test asserts state.json absent, live canonical/ directory empty, no leftover .import_pending_*. Zip handling is structurally safe: validate_pair_id enforces canonical UUIDv4 on every zip entry filename, so no slug derived from archive content can ever contain a path separator, '..', or absolute prefix. Residuals: CQ-06 export_to_zip still catches BaseException to unlink the tmp file. CQ-09 winerror branches still have zero Linux-runnable test. CQ-10/CQ-13 parameter counts in parse_rules_md (8) and parse_slash_command_markdown (5) unchanged. CQ-12 downgraded to INFO (only one underscore-prefixed import remains: _normalize_toml_text from codex_io). New CQ-14: portable_archive.py grew to 616 lines (over 300-line soft limit).

findings.description: import_from_zip is still ~155 lines, exceeding the 40-line guideline. Phase A/B/C structure makes responsibilities clearly partitioned but the function body remains long. Tool-side projection is still not staged in Phase 1 (documented caveat).
findings.recommendation: Track tool-side projection staging as a separate refactor item; consider extracting Phase C body into _project_decision(state, decision, ...) to bring import_from_zip back under 80 lines.

findings.description: Persisting: export_to_zip still does `except BaseException: tmp_path.unlink(missing_ok=True); raise`. SystemExit and KeyboardInterrupt are caught for the duration of the unlink. The raise re-propagates, so blast radius is bounded, but a tighter `except (Exception, KeyboardInterrupt)` would be cleaner.
findings.recommendation: Replace with try/finally that always tries to unlink the tmp path on exit, or narrow the catch to Exception.

findings.description: Persisting: the winerror branch in is_transient_fs_error (winerror ∈ {5, 32, 33}) has no Linux-runnable test. test_filesystem_retry_helper.py only exercises the PermissionError branch.
findings.recommendation: Add one test that synthesises OSError with .winerror = 32 and asserts retry_fs retries it.

findings.description: parse_rules_md still takes 8 parameters. canonical_name and artifact_path overlap in purpose.
findings.recommendation: Collapse canonical_name + artifact_path into a single RulesIdentity (path, override_name) data class.

findings.description: Improved: was five-plus underscore-prefixed imports; now one (_normalize_toml_text from codex_io). rules_io.py no longer crosses any module-private boundary.
findings.recommendation: Promote codex_io._normalize_toml_text to a public name or move into a shared toml_normalization module.

findings.description: parse_slash_command_markdown still has 5 parameters. On the threshold rather than over it.
findings.recommendation: Same as CQ-10 — collapse identity parameters into a small dataclass once a third parser is added.

findings.category: M1 module length over soft limit
findings.description: New: portable_archive.py is now 616 lines, exceeding the 300-line module guideline. The module remains topically coherent (export + import + collision policy + staging).
findings.recommendation: Consider splitting into portable_archive/{__init__,export,import_,collision,staging}.py once the file crosses 700 lines.
